scene.summary: Multi-layer scene building blocks: backdrops, title stacks, camera rigs

layer.summary: Individual layer creation and manipulation: footage import, shape layers

effect.summary: Visual effects applied as adjustment layers: grain, flicker, glow, gate weave, etc.

preset.summary: Compound actions composing multiple blocks into curated looks or scenes

comp.summary: Composition-level operations: create comp, add guides, settings

utility.summary: Data processing, timing calculations, and non-AE helpers

actions.name: Add Backdrop
actions.description: Creates a three-layer atmospheric backdrop: dark base solid, warm accent band, bright signal line.
actions.inputs: comp: CompItem; opts: { } (no opts for v1 — defaults are opinionated)
actions.outputs: Array<AVLayer> — [bg, warmBand, topLine]
actions.whenToUse: You want a quick tone-setting backdrop for a demo or presentation comp.
actions.example: var layers = addBackdrop(comp);

actions.name: Add Beat Markers
actions.description: Places evenly-spaced comp markers from t=0 through comp.duration, labeled "Beat 1", "Beat 2", etc.
actions.inputs: comp: CompItem; opts: { spacingSeconds: number (default 1.0, minimum 0.25) }
actions.outputs: number — count of markers added
actions.whenToUse: You want a regular beat grid on the timeline for timing reference or music sync.
actions.example: var count = addBeatMarkers(comp, { spacingSeconds: 0.5 });

actions.name: Add Camera
actions.description: Creates a camera layer with configurable zoom and depth of field settings.
actions.inputs: comp: CompItem; opts: { name: string (default "Camera"), zoom: number (optional, pixels — AE default if omitted), depthOfField: boolean (default false) }
actions.outputs: CameraLayer — the newly created camera layer
actions.whenToUse: You need a standalone camera layer without a full orbit rig. Use addCameraRig for an animated orbit rig.
actions.example: var cam = addCamera(comp, { name: "Main Camera", depthOfField: true });

actions.name: Add Camera Rig
actions.description: Adds an orbit camera rig: a 3D camera parented to a null controller with a gentle Y-rotation animation.
actions.inputs: comp: CompItem; opts: { } (no opts for v1)
actions.outputs: Array<AVLayer> — [camera, controller]
actions.whenToUse: You want a 3D camera that slowly orbits the scene over the comp's duration.
actions.example: var layers = addCameraRig(comp);

actions.name: Add Color Grade
actions.description: Adds a color grade adjustment layer: Glow + Vignette + Levels stack for a warm, contrast-crushed analog look.
actions.inputs: comp: CompItem; opts: { glowIntensity: number (default 0.2, Glow intensity 0-1), vignetteAmount: number (default 50, CS Vignette amount 0-100) }
actions.outputs: AVLayer — the adjustment layer
actions.whenToUse: You want a subtle warm glow, vignette, and crushed levels for an analog or cinematic color treatment.
actions.example: var layer = addColorGrade(comp);

actions.name: Add Expression Control
actions.description: Adds an expression control effect to a layer (Slider, Angle, Checkbox, Color, Point, 3D Point, or Dropdown). Maps type strings to correct ADBE match names and sets the initial value on the correct sub-property.
actions.inputs: layer: AVLayer; opts: { type: "slider"|"angle"|"checkbox"|"color"|"point"|"point3d"|"dropdown" (required), name: string (optional display name), value:
actions.outputs: PropertyGroup — the newly applied expression control effect
actions.whenToUse: You need a slider, color picker, checkbox, or other control on a null or adjustment layer for expression linking.
actions.example: var ctrl = addExpressionControl(nullLayer, { type: "slider", name: "Speed", value: 50 });

actions.name: Add Expression Rig
actions.description: Creates a null controller layer loaded with expression control effects (sliders, colors, checkboxes, etc.) for driving expressions across a comp. Returns the null layer and an array of the applied control effects.
actions.inputs: comp: CompItem; opts: { controls: Array<{ name: string, type: "slider"|"angle"|"checkbox"|"color"|"point"|"point3d"|"dropdown", value:
actions.outputs: Object — { null: AVLayer, controls: PropertyGroup[] }
actions.whenToUse: You need a single controller null with multiple expression controls so expressions throughout the comp can reference one authoritative source.
actions.example: var rig = addExpressionRig(comp, { controls: [{ name: "Speed", type: "slider", value: 50 }, { name: "Primary Color", type: "color", value: [1, 0.5, 0] }] });

actions.name: Add Film Damage Treatment
actions.description: Composes gate_weave + grain + flicker + color_grade into a four-layer film damage look.
actions.inputs: comp: CompItem; opts: { } (no opts for v1 — pass opts to individual blocks for fine control)
actions.outputs: Object — { gateWeave, grain, flicker, colorGrade } (the four adjustment layers)
actions.whenToUse: You want an instant analog/film-damage look applied on top of existing comp layers without choosing individual effects.
actions.example: var layers = addFilmDamageTreatment(comp);

actions.name: Add Flicker
actions.description: Adds a flicker adjustment layer: an Exposure effect with a wiggle expression to simulate random brightness fluctuation.
actions.inputs: comp: CompItem; opts: { speed: number (default 12, wiggle frequency in Hz), amount: number (default 0.1, wiggle amplitude in stops) }
actions.outputs: AVLayer — the adjustment layer
actions.whenToUse: You want random brightness flicker simulating aging film or unstable lighting.
actions.example: var layer = addFlicker(comp);

actions.name: Add Gate Weave
actions.description: Adds a gate-weave adjustment layer: a Transform effect with wiggle expressions on position and scale to simulate film gate instability.
actions.inputs: comp: CompItem; opts: { intensity: number (default 1.0, scale multiplier for wiggle amounts) }
actions.outputs: AVLayer — the adjustment layer
actions.whenToUse: You want subtle horizontal/vertical camera shake simulating a film gate slipping in the projector gate.
actions.example: var layer = addGateWeave(comp);

actions.name: Add Grain
actions.description: Adds a grain adjustment layer: Noise + Gaussian Blur + Unsharp Mask stack to simulate analog film grain.
actions.inputs: comp: CompItem; opts: { amount: number (default 10, Noise amount 0-100), blurRadius: number (default 6, Gaussian Blur blurriness) }
actions.outputs: AVLayer — the adjustment layer
actions.whenToUse: You want organic film-style grain on a comp without a full film damage treatment.
actions.example: var layer = addGrain(comp);

actions.name: Add Guide Preset
actions.description: Adds a named group of compositional guides to a comp (thirds, safe margins, or center cross).
actions.inputs: comp: CompItem; opts: { preset: "thirds"|"safe"|"center" }
actions.outputs: number — count of guides added (0 if preset name is unrecognized)
actions.whenToUse: You need standard guide layouts in a comp. Requires AE 16.1+ (comp.addGuide).
actions.example: var count = addGuidePreset(comp, { preset: "thirds" });

actions.name: Add Light
actions.description: Creates a light layer of the specified type (point, spot, parallel, ambient) with configurable intensity, color, and shadow options.
actions.inputs: comp: CompItem; opts: { type: "point"|"spot"|"parallel"|"ambient" (default "point"), name: string (default "Light"), position: [x,y,z] (default comp center, z=-600), intensity: number (default 100), color: [r,g,b] (default [1,1,1]), coneAngle: number (spot only, default 90), castsShadows: boolean (default false) }
actions.outputs: LightLayer — the newly created light layer
actions.whenToUse: You need a 3D scene light. Maps type strings to LightType enum; only sets cone angle for spot lights.
actions.example: var light = addLight(comp, { type: "spot", intensity: 120, coneAngle: 60, castsShadows: true });

actions.name: Add Mask
actions.description: Adds a mask to a layer with rectangle, ellipse, or custom bezier shape. Uses verified ADBE match names (ADBE Mask Parade, ADBE Mask Atom, ADBE Mask Shape, ADBE Mask Feather, ADBE Mask Opacity, ADBE Mask Offset).
actions.inputs: layer: AVLayer; opts: { shape: "rectangle"|"ellipse"|"custom" (default "rectangle"), rect: [x,y,w,h] (rectangle/ellipse, default full comp bounds), vertices: [x,y][] (custom only), inTangents: [x,y][] (custom), outTangents: [x,y][] (custom), mode: "add"|"subtract"|"intersect"|"lighten"|"darken"|"difference"|"none" (default "add"), feather: [fx,fy] (default [0,0]), opacity: number (0–100, default 100), expansion: number (default 0), inverted: boolean (default false), name: string (optional display name) }
actions.outputs: MaskPropertyGroup — the newly created mask group
actions.whenToUse: You need to add a rectangular, elliptical, or custom bezier mask to any layer. Coordinates are in comp space.
actions.example: var mask = addMask(layer, { shape: "rectangle", rect: [100, 100, 800, 400], feather: [20, 20] });

actions.name: Add Null
actions.description: Creates a null object layer for use as a controller, parent anchor, or expression target.
actions.inputs: comp: CompItem; opts: { name: string (default "Null Controller"), position: [x,y] (default comp center), is3D: boolean (default false) }
actions.outputs: AVLayer — the newly created null layer
actions.whenToUse: You need an invisible controller layer for parenting, expressions, or camera rigs.
actions.example: var ctrl = addNull(comp, { name: "Camera CTRL", is3D: true });

actions.name: Add Shape Layer
actions.description: Creates a shape layer with rectangle, ellipse, polygon, or star geometry, plus optional fill and stroke. Uses correct ADBE match-name nesting to avoid property-path pitfalls.
actions.inputs: comp: CompItem; opts: { name: string, position: [x,y] (default comp center), shape: "rectangle"|"ellipse"|"polygon"|"star" (default "rectangle"), size: [w,h] (default [200,200]), points: number (polygon/star), innerRadius: number (star), outerRadius: number (star/polygon), fillColor: [r,g,b] (null = no fill), strokeColor: [r,g,b] (null = no stroke), strokeWidth: number (default 2), roundness: number (rectangle only) }
actions.outputs: ShapeLayer — the newly created shape layer
actions.whenToUse: You need a shape layer with a specific geometry. Handles correct ADBE nesting and sets star type before inner radius to prevent AE errors.
actions.example: var layer = addShapeLayer(comp, { shape: "rectangle", size: [400, 200], fillColor: [0.2, 0.4, 1] });

actions.name: Add Solid
actions.description: Creates a solid color layer with configurable color, dimensions, duration, and adjustment layer mode.
actions.inputs: comp: CompItem; opts: { color: [r,g,b] (default [1,1,1]), name: string (default "Solid"), width: number (default comp.width), height: number (default comp.height), duration: number (default comp.duration), isAdjustment: boolean (default false) }
actions.outputs: AVLayer — the newly created solid layer
actions.whenToUse: You need a solid color layer for backgrounds, mattes, color fills, or adjustment layer bases.
actions.example: var layer = addSolid(comp, { color: [0.07, 0.09, 0.14], name: "BG Dark", isAdjustment: false });

actions.name: Add Star Trim Animation
actions.description: Adds an animated 5-point star shape layer with a trim-paths draw-on animation over 1 second.
actions.inputs: comp: CompItem; opts: { points: number, innerRadius: number, outerRadius: number } (all optional)
actions.outputs: ShapeLayer — the newly created star layer
actions.whenToUse: You want a shape-layer star with a trim-paths reveal animation as a motion design element.
actions.example: var layer = addStarTrimAnimation(comp);

actions.name: Add Text Animator
actions.description: Adds a text animator preset to a text layer: typeOn (opacity reveal), fadeUp (opacity + position), trackingIn (tracking collapse), or scaleBounce (scale reveal with overshoot). Uses Range Selectors and keyframes for time-based animation.
actions.inputs: layer: TextLayer; opts: { preset: "typeOn"|"fadeUp"|"trackingIn"|"scaleBounce" (default "typeOn"), duration: number (seconds, default 1.0), stagger: number (0-1 smoothness, default 0.05) }
actions.outputs: PropertyGroup — the newly added text animator
actions.whenToUse: You need a quick entrance animation on a text layer without manually building animators and range selectors.
actions.example: var anim = addTextAnimator(layer, { preset: "fadeUp", duration: 0.8 });

actions.name: Add Text Layer
actions.description: Creates a text layer with full styling control. Applies all required AE TextDocument rules: applyFill before fillColor, explicit justification, anchor reset to [0,0], full property path access.
actions.inputs: comp: CompItem; opts: { text: string (default ""), fontSize: number (default 48), fillColor: [r,g,b] (default [1,1,1]), font: string, justification: "left"|"center"|"right" (default "center"), position: [x,y] (default comp center), name: string, tracking: number, leading: number }
actions.outputs: TextLayer — the newly created text layer
actions.whenToUse: You need a text layer with reliable styling. Prevents common pitfalls: applyFill, justification, anchor drift, and property path issues.
actions.example: var layer = addTextLayer(comp, { text: "Hello World", fontSize: 72, fillColor: [1, 1, 0], justification: "center" });

actions.name: Add Title Stack
actions.description: Adds a two-layer animated title stack (hero title + subtitle) with fade and slide-up keyframes.
actions.inputs: comp: CompItem; opts: { title: string, subtitle: string } (both optional — defaults to demo copy)
actions.outputs: Array<TextLayer> — [titleLayer, subtitleLayer]
actions.whenToUse: You want a quick hero text layout with entrance animation for demo or presentation comps.
actions.example: var layers = addTitleStack(comp, { title: "My Project", subtitle: "A short description" });

actions.name: Apply Effect
actions.description: Applies an effect to a layer by ADBE match name, then sets any specified property values. Validates the layer type and provides a clear error if the effect match name is not found (e.g. missing third-party plugin).
actions.inputs: layer: AVLayer; opts: { matchName: string (required, ADBE match name), properties: Object (matchName or display name -> value pairs, optional), name: string (optional display name override) }
actions.outputs: PropertyGroup — the newly applied effect group
actions.whenToUse: You need to apply any built-in or third-party effect to a layer by match name. Use this instead of hard-coding effect application logic. Warn the user if their project may not have required third-party plugins.
actions.example: var blur = applyEffect(layer, { matchName: "ADBE Gaussian Blur 2", properties: { "ADBE Gaussian Blur 2-0001": 20 } });

actions.name: Build Demo Scene
actions.description: Creates a fully composed demo scene: new comp + backdrop + guides (thirds + safe) + title stack + camera rig + beat markers.
actions.inputs: opts: { name: string, width: number, height: number, duration: number, fps: number, spacingSeconds: number }
actions.outputs: CompItem — the newly created composition
actions.whenToUse: You want to build a complete demo comp with all standard building blocks in one call.
actions.example: var comp = buildDemoScene({ name: "Demo", width: 1920, height: 1080, duration: 8, fps: 24, spacingSeconds: 1 });

actions.name: Build Motion Graphic Scene
actions.description: Creates a complete motion graphic comp: background solid, title + subtitle text layers, accent shapes, and guide preset. Three style tokens control the palette and geometry.
actions.inputs: opts: { name: string (default "MG Scene"), width: number (default 1920), height: number (default 1080), duration: number (default 8), fps: number (default 24), title: string (default "Title"), subtitle: string (default "Subtitle"), bgColor: [r,g,b] (overrides style default), style: "minimal"|"bold"|"tech" (default "minimal") }
actions.outputs: Object — { comp, bg, title, subtitle, accent } (layers may be null if creation fails)
actions.whenToUse: You need a production-ready motion graphic starting point without assembling every layer individually. Pick a style token and supply copy.
actions.example: var scene = buildMotionGraphicScene({ title: "Q4 Review", subtitle: "Sales & Marketing", style: "bold" });

actions.name: Create Comp
actions.description: Creates a new composition in the project with a dark background and opens it in the viewer.
actions.inputs: opts: { name: string, width: number, height: number, duration: number, fps: number }
actions.outputs: CompItem — the newly created composition
actions.whenToUse: You need to create a new comp programmatically with specific dimensions. Does NOT require an existing comp.
actions.example: var comp = createCompFromValues({ name: "My Comp", width: 1920, height: 1080, duration: 8, fps: 24 });

actions.name: Data Timing
actions.description: Calculates and recalculates display durations for data-driven text elements using a words-per-second model. Exposes three primary functions: computeDurationFromText, resolveElementDuration, recalculateDurationsOnJson.
actions.inputs: data: Object (with elements/messages array); targetSecondsPerWord: number; targetMinDuration: number
actions.outputs: boolean — true if any durations were changed
actions.whenToUse: Display duration should vary based on text length (subtitle timing, auto-paced slideshows, narration sync).
actions.example: var changed = recalculateDurationsOnJson(data, 0.38, 1.5);

actions.name: Ensure Project Folder
actions.description: Finds or creates a folder in the AE Project panel. Supports nested paths using "/" as a separator (e.g. "Assets/Images" creates "Assets" at the root, then "Images" inside it). A parentFolder can be passed to resolve the path relative to an existing folder.
actions.inputs: opts: { name: string (required, may contain "/" for nested paths), parentFolder: FolderItem (default: project root) }
actions.outputs: FolderItem — the deepest folder in the path (created if it did not exist)
actions.whenToUse: You need to organize imported footage, comps, or other items into project panel folders. Safely re-uses existing folders; never creates duplicates.
actions.example: var folder = ensureProjectFolder({ name: "Assets/Images" });

actions.name: Image Swap
actions.description: Imports footage files, replaces layer sources, and scales layers to fit or fill a comp. Exposes five functions: replaceLayerSource, fitLayerToComp, getOrImportFootage, importFolderAssets, ensureProjectFolder.
actions.inputs: comp: CompItem; opts: { layerNameOrIndex: string|number, footageFile: File, fitMode: "fit"|"fill" }
actions.outputs: boolean — true if the source was successfully replaced
actions.whenToUse: You need to import external images and swap layer sources with proper fit/fill scaling.
actions.example: replaceLayerSource(comp, { layerNameOrIndex: "Photo", footageFile: new File("/path/img.jpg"), fitMode: "fill" });

actions.name: Pre Compose
actions.description: Pre-composes a set of layers within a comp by 1-based index. Sorts indices ascending (required by AE), validates range, and returns the new CompItem.
actions.inputs: comp: CompItem; opts: { layerIndices: number[] (required, 1-based), name: string (default "Pre-comp"), moveAttributes: boolean (default true) }
actions.outputs: CompItem — the newly created pre-composition
actions.whenToUse: You need to collapse layers into a nested comp programmatically. Always pass indices as 1-based integers.
actions.example: var precomp = preCompose(comp, { layerIndices: [1, 2, 3], name: "BG Elements" });

actions.name: Queue Comp
actions.description: Adds a comp to the After Effects render queue.
actions.inputs: comp: CompItem
actions.outputs: undefined
actions.whenToUse: You want to programmatically send a comp to the render queue without touching the UI.
actions.example: queueComp(comp);

actions.name: Set Expression
actions.description: Applies an expression string to an animatable property with three-guard validation: PROPERTY type check, canSetExpression check, and expression string check.
actions.inputs: prop: Property; opts: { expression: string (required) }
actions.outputs: boolean — true if the expression was applied successfully
actions.whenToUse: You need to apply an expression to a property. Guards against non-property groups and properties that don't support expressions.
actions.example: var ok = setExpression(layer.property("Transform").property("Opacity"), { expression: "wiggle(2, 10)" });

actions.name: Set Keyframes
actions.description: Sets one or more keyframes on an animatable property, with configurable easing. Validates canVaryOverTime, sorts keyframes by time, and maps easing strings to KeyframeInterpolationType + KeyframeEase.
actions.inputs: prop: Property; opts: { keyframes: Array<{time: number, value:
actions.outputs: number — count of keyframes added
actions.whenToUse: You need to set keyframe values on any animatable property with consistent easing. Handles both temporal and spatial properties.
actions.example: var count = setKeyframes(layer.property("Transform").property("Opacity"), { keyframes: [{time: 0, value: 0}, {time: 1, value: 100}], easing: "easeInOut" });

actions.name: Set Layer Parent
actions.description: Sets the parent of a child layer within a comp by layer name or index. Pass null as parent to unparent. Prevents self-parenting.
actions.inputs: comp: CompItem; opts: { child: string|number (layer name or 1-based index, required), parent: string|number|null (layer name or 1-based index; null to unparent) }
actions.outputs: boolean — true if the parent was set (or cleared) successfully
actions.whenToUse: You need to parent one layer to another (e.g. camera to null, logo to controller). Use null as parent to break an existing parent link.
actions.example: setLayerParent(comp, { child: "Logo", parent: "Null Controller" });
